+    def delete_oldest_application_instance (self) :
+        """
+            Finds and deletes the oldest application instance
+            """
+        if not self.application_instances:
+            return
+    
+        # Find the scope_id with the smallest last_used timestamp
+        oldest_scope_id = min(
+            self.application_instances.keys(),
+            key=lambda scope_id: self.application_instances[scope_id]["last_used"]
         )
-        for scope_id, details in self.application_instances.items():
-            if details["last_used"] == oldest_time:
-                self.delete_application_instance(scope_id)
-                # Return to make sure we only delete one in case two have
-                # the same oldest time
-                return
-
+    
+        # Delete the oldest application instance
+        self.delete_application_instance(oldest_scope_id)

diff --git a/asgiref/sync.py b/asgiref/sync.py
index 54496ef..591e781 100644
--- a/asgiref/sync.py
+++ b/asgiref/sync.py
@@ -41,17 +41,19 @@ _P = ParamSpec("_P")
 _R = TypeVar("_R")
 
 
-def _restore_context(context: contextvars.Context) -> None:
-    # Check for changes in contextvars, and set them to the current
-    # context for downstream consumers
-    for cvar in context:
-        cvalue = context.get(cvar)
-        try:
-            if cvar.get() != cvalue:
-                cvar.set(cvalue)
-        except LookupError:
-            cvar.set(cvalue)
-
+def _restore_context (context: contextvars.Context) -> None : # Check for changes in contextvars, and set them to the current # context for downstream consumers
+    # Get the current context
+    current_context = contextvars.copy_context()
+    
+    # Iterate through all context variables in the saved context
+    for var in context:
+        # Get the value from both contexts
+        saved_value = context.get(var)
+        current_value = current_context.get(var)
+        
+        # If the value has changed in the saved context, update it in the current context
+        if saved_value != current_value:
+            var.set(saved_value)
 
 # Python 3.12 deprecates asyncio.iscoroutinefunction() as an alias for
 # inspect.iscoroutinefunction(), whilst also removing the _is_coroutine marker.
